raw_consumer_statement: A dispute on a company for {$4400.00} was initiated on XX/XX/XXXX. I made the order from that company 2 weeks before and they never sent me any information about an order confirmation or tracking number or anything of that nature. They did not even ask me for a signature on delivery for such an extremely large order. I never received my order after waiting 2 weeks and getting no support or updates from the company I purchased it for so I finally decide to initiate a dispute with my Amex card since the company never shipped my items. Amex at first gave me a temporary credit for this order. I was very pleased with the outcome. Later on, the company started talking to Amex saying they shipped my order but not telling me anything. Where was this information when I made the order? I would've taken a day off of work to get this order and sign for it because this was a large amount in savings for me but no I get no communication from the company that I made an order and it shipped and no tracking number or signature required. The company provides amex some random tracking number and shipping information which doesn't even look like mine and Amex sided with them. I still have yet to receive any of my packages. I am confused and saddened by this and I reopened the dispute explaining that I was never given any information on this order and Amex once again ruled in the favor of the company. This is very unfortunate. I thought Amex was suppose to protect it's customers but I wasn't protected from a very shady company that did not ship my items to me and take my money. This is not the experience I want from Amex and I am very dissapointed and disheartened by this whole situation as a cardholder of 5 years.
